best: Notes the DurationUnit-based API is unusable from multiplatform code unless callers use okhttp3.internal.TimeUnit, so the methods likely must be doubled.
Suggests extracting shared reload utilities into a function-utils SharedUtils rather than pulsar-common, but self-defers the broader dedup as out of scope (organizational, not a defect).
Flags that File/URI converters should follow the trimming BuiltInConverter pattern, catching the inconsistency that the File converter omits trimming.
Suggests the old config file should pass through while the new config only sets the app name, a backward-compat design preference not verifiable from the shown diff.
Questions whether extensions actually bytecode-instrument JUnit annotations or if pre-discovery class loading is abundance-of-caution; a scoping question, not a concrete defect.
Shows the size-class scheme is broken because adjacent classes (e.g. 640) collapse into the same power-of-2 bucket as 512/1024, plus the 528-byte index-mapping memory cost.
Identifies a non-obvious semantic of the flipped boolean: the createRequirement method also makes the requirement greedy, confirming the change matches the desired behavior.
